mnemonics: Think 'SHARED' for Virtualized DC Architecture: Servers, Hypervisor, Abstraction, Resources, Elasticity, Dynamic
keyPoints: The hypervisor is the core software that enables virtualization by abstracting physical hardware.
Resource pooling allows for the dynamic and on-demand allocation of compute, storage, and networking.
The architecture enables multi-tenancy, where multiple independent users share the same physical infrastructure securely.
commonMistakes: Assuming virtualization automatically provides security; proper isolation and configuration are critical.
Believing all resources are dedicated; a core feature is the sharing and over-provisioning of pooled resources.
